title: Seeded Recursive Backtracking Generation · Behavior Case: Generation Validation
summary: Generation chooses visited neighbors, recursive depth overflows silently, random state is global, and completion is confused with connectivity. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the generation validation increment in seeded iterative and recursive backtracker. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify all cells visited, edge count, reciprocal walls, connectivity, acyclicity, seed replay, and changed dimensions.
Compare recursive and explicit-stack backtracking for clarity, depth, pause, serialization, and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Generation Validation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to seeded iterative and recursive backtracker.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-backtracker-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_generation_backtracker_5(cells: int = 48, visited: int = 48, carved_edges: int = 47, stack_peak: int = 21, replay_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0:
        return False, visited, "generation-count"
    if visited != cells:
        return False, visited, "unvisited-cell"
    if carved_edges != cells - 1:
        return False, carved_edges, "tree-shape"
    if stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match:
        return False, stack_peak, "replay-invariant"
    assert visited == cells and carved_edges == cells - 1
    return True, visited, "seeded-backtracker"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify all cells visited, edge count, reciprocal walls, connectivity, acyclicity, seed replay, and changed dimensions.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Generation Validation model · Theory Generation Valida…, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Generation Validation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Generation Validation model · Theory Generation Valida…, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2347 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seeded backtracking progress, completion, topology, depth, and replay; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify all cells visited, edge count, reciprocal walls, connectivity, acyclicity, seed replay, and changed dimensions.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Generation Validation model · Theory Generation Valida…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Reaching the initial cell again proves generation completed correctly.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-generation-validation-u9we91.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-generation-validation-u9we91(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_generation_validation_u9we91\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or visited < 0 or carved_edges < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visited != cells)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?carved_edges != cells - 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stack_peak < 1 or not replay_match)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
